data.title: Dollar Loses 0.46% to 1.3782 Canadian Dollars — Data Talk
data.description:

data.title: Consumer Cos Up, But Weekly Gains Limited on Rate Outlook — Consumer Roundup
data.description:
Consumer companies rose, but remained slightly lower for the week, amid uncertainty about how long the Federal Reserve's rate-cutting path would last.

"The Fed remains in data-dependent mode, awaiting evidence that either growth or inflation deserves more attention than the other," said Naomi Fink, chief global strategist at money manager Amova Asset Management. Amova's key takeaway from Fed Chairman Jerome Powell's comments on the Fed action was the caveat that there was now "no risk-free paths" for the central bank.

The SPDR S&P Homebuilders exchange-traded fund rose modestly for the week.

Late Thursday, Lennar fell for a fourth consecutive quarter as it continues to offer aggressive incentives to stimulate sales in a dormant housing market.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Energy Down With Oil Futures — Energy Roundup
data.description:
Shares of energy companies fell alongside oil futures.

The Federal Reserve cut interest rates midweek, but Fed Chairman Jerome Powell warned that there were risks on both the demand and inflation front.

Oil traders are also attempting to handicap the likely impact of sanctions on Russian oil. Traders appeared to shrug off new sanctions from the European Union. The Trump administration, meanwhile, appears reluctant to impose new sanctions.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Metis to Participate in Korea Blockchain Week in Seoul on September 22nd
data.description:
Metis has announced its official partnership with Korea Blockchain Week 2025, scheduled to be held in Seoul, from September 22 to 28.

Refer to the official tweet by METIS:

Metis🌿
@MetisL2

Metis & @LazAINetwork are official partners of @kbwofficial 🇰🇷

Sept 22ndâ€“28th, weâ€™re in Seoul for Korea Blockchain Week, shaping whatâ€™s next for blockchain and AI.

Come and say GMetis to us at #KBW2025! pic.twitter.com/49PAMvdNbn

Sep 19, 2025

METIS Info

Metis is a Layer 2 Rollup platform built on the Ethereum network that aims to tackle some of the key challenges faced by the main net Ethereum — namely transaction speed, cost, and scalability. It achieves this by offering a framework for simple, rapid deployment of smart contracts within the network, thereby facilitating seamless and efficient operations on the Ethereum blockchain.

At the core of Metis’s offerings are several key features: Metis Oracles, Metis Nodes, and Metis SubGraph. Metis Oracles provide real-time data from external sources, connecting blockchains to external systems and supplying smart contracts with crucial outside information. Metis Nodes, which store a complete copy of the distributed network, enhance security and reliability. Lastly, the Metis SubGraph serves as an indexing protocol for gathering, processing, and storing data for use in the blockchain network, aiming to ease information retrieval through a service known as GraphQL. These combined features position Metis as a significant player in improving the functionality and performance of the Ethereum network.

METIS is an ERC-20 governance token compatible with the Ethereum blockchain network. The main use cases for the METIS token are paying transaction fees on the Metis network and staking.

data.title: Trump says his administration will hold news conference on autism next week
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump said on Friday his administration would hold a news conference next week to make an announcement regarding autism.

"We're going to have a news conference early next week on autism, and I think you're going to see some things that we learned," Trump told reporters in the Oval Office.

"Autism is totally out of control, and one of the biggest, one of the most important things that in my opinion I had to do is to see what's going on with autism," Trump said.

The Wall Street Journal reported earlier this month that U.S. Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. plans to announce that use of Kenvue's popular over-the-counter pain medication Tylenol in pregnant women is potentially linked to autism, contrary to medical guidelines that say it is safe to use.

data.title: UK's Starmer to press ahead with digital ID plans, FT reports
data.description:
Key points:
  • Digital ID proposal part of broader immigration system reform -FT
  • Announcement expected at party conference, details still being finalized -FT
  • Government committed to tech for service access -spokesperson

British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is moving forward with plans to introduce digital identification systems as part of efforts to overhaul the country's asylum and immigration system, with an announcement anticipated as early as his party's conference this month, the Financial Times reported on Friday.

The finer details of the scheme were still being ironed out and the timeline could change, the report added, citing two people briefed on the matter.

Immigration and asylum remain at the heart of Britain's political debate, with the government under pressure to curb record migrant arrivals in small boats, while also addressing the strain of housing tens of thousands of asylum seekers.

One option under consideration would give digital IDs to all people legally entitled to reside in Britain, whether citizens or those with legal immigration status, the Financial Times said.

The digital ID could be used for employment verification and rental agreements, though the government may still narrow the scope or revisit the plan, the newspaper added.

A government spokesperson said Britain was committed to expanding the use of technology to make it easier for people to access services, pointing to existing systems such as e-visas and the NHS app.

"We will look at any serious proposals that would help people access public services, including digital ID," the spokesperson said in an emailed statement to Reuters.

data.title: In anti-ICE protest, Illinois Democrats tear-gassed, pepper-sprayed by federal agents
data.description:
By Heather Schlitz

In a chaotic scene at an immigration detention facility in the Chicago suburbs, Illinois Democratic candidates running for Congress said they were tear-gassed, shot with pepper balls and shoved to the ground by federal agents on Friday while protesting against President Donald Trump's immigration surge into the city.

Since a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement raids began earlier this month, protesters have gathered outside the facility in Broadview, Illinois, in mostly peaceful demonstrations. But the standoff with ICE agents sharply escalated on Friday, with agents body-slamming protesters, firing tear gas and releasing pepper balls that coated protesters in a cloud of white powder.

Kat Abughazaleh, a Democratic congressional candidate, said she was tear-gassed and shoved twice by federal agents while sitting down with other protesters to block vehicle access to the facility on Friday morning. Abughazaleh posted footage on social media that showed heavily armed agents lifting her and throwing her onto the ground, which she said injured her hand and left her with large bruises on her right side.

"It was painful, but it could have been worse," she said.

Two large-scale immigration enforcement operations that began in early September have been sharply criticized by top Illinois officials, including Governor J.B. Pritzker and Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson.

In a statement following the protests, the U.S. Department of Homeland Security said that "rioters assaulted law enforcement, threw tear gas cans, slashed tires of cars, blocked the entrance of the building, and trespassed on private property."

Three people were arrested, the agency said.

In a separate incident on Thursday, Democratic politicians in New York were arrested during a protest at a building used by ICE.

Daniel Biss, the mayor of Evanston, an affluent Chicago suburb, who is also running for Congress, said he was also hit by tear gas while protesting with dozens of others outside the facility.

"I was totally unable to breathe and eventually found myself stumbling over on the ground trying to regain my breath," he said.

A team of volunteer medics washed the tear gas off his face, guided him into a recovery pose to help him breathe easier and advised him on how to safely handle his contaminated clothes, he said.

One protester, in swim goggles, an N95 mask and a T-shirt that said "Vote," carried an American flag and a sign that in recent weeks has appeared on front lawns and apartment windows across the third-largest city in the United States that read: "Hands off Chicago."

data.title: Stocks finish week higher; Wall Street at record highs
data.description:
Key points:
  • Wall Street stocks advance in choppy trading
  • Fed rate cut keeps investors upbeat
  • European shares finish week slightly lower
  • Gold prices rise, crude oil falls

By Chibuike Oguh and Elizabeth Howcroft

Global stocks rose in choppy trading on Friday, on track for a weekly gain, driven by positive sentiment on Wall Street following key central bank decisions.

The Federal Reserve cut U.S. interest rates by a quarter of a percentage point on Wednesday, the first easing since December, while Norway and Canada also cut rates.

On Wall Street, all three indexes closed at record highs. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.37% to 46,315.27, the S&P 500 advanced 0.49% to 6,664.36, and the Nasdaq Composite firmed 0.72% to 22,631.48. All three also hit record highs the day before.

European shares finished down 0.16%, and for the week were down 0.13%.

Japan's Nikkei fell 0.57% after the Bank of Japan decided to start selling its holdings of risky assets. MSCI's gauge of stocks across the globe EURONEXT:IACWI hit a fresh record high of 982.29, adding nearly 1% for the week.

Investors are betting that central bank rate cuts will boost stocks further.

"The market for the past several weeks has all been focused on and relying on the Fed meeting and the Fed's decision, and there was enough in the decision to leave everyone just slightly disappointed though basically satisfied," said Michael Farr, chief executive of investment advisory firm Farr, Miller & Washington in Washington.

The Fed stopped short of endorsing market expectations for a clear string of rate cuts, emphasizing a meeting-by-meeting, data-dependent approach. The Fed's tone, along with the wide range of views within the U.S. central bank, disappointed some investors, who had hoped the stock market would be boosted by a rapid shift to lower rates, analysts said.

"Markets have done exceptionally well and now markets are looking for the next driver or the next bit of news," Farr said. "I think as we probably get into earnings season in October, those reports will be more important than ever because we need to see and the Fed needs to see if tariffs are indeed making their way into bottom-line profits."

The yield on benchmark U.S. 10-year notes rose 2.5 basis points to 4.129%. The 2-year note (US2YT=RR) yield, which typically moves in step with interest rate expectations for the Fed, rose 0.6 basis points to 3.574%.

"The short-term momentum is clearly higher and we are playing the market to the upside because that's what the short-term momentum is telling you," said Bill Strazzullo, partner and chief market strategist at Bell Curve Trading in Boston.

"But I think it's a lot more complicated now because the Fed clearly has chosen to err on the side of the weaker labor market so that's talking about more rate cuts."

Following their first call in three months, U.S. President Donald Trump said he and Chinese President Xi Jinping made progress on a TikTok agreement and would meet face-to-face in six weeks in South Korea to discuss trade, illicit drugs and Russia's war in Ukraine.

A stopgap spending bill that would avert an October 1 government shutdown fell short in the U.S. Senate on Friday. The bill had been passed by the House of Representatives.

The U.S. dollar index rose for a third straight session, up 0.33% to 97.67, but was still set to notch a third straight week of losses.

The dollar strengthened 0.4% to 0.795 against the Swiss franc , but was down 0.03% to 147.97 against the Japanese yen .

The euro fell 0.35% against the dollar to $1.1745.

The British pound fell 0.64% to $1.3467 .

The Bank of England kept rates on hold on Thursday, but slowed the pace at which it is unloading the government bonds it purchased in previous crises.

Oil prices settled lower as traders' worries about fuel demand outweighed the typical boost from a U.S. rate cut.

Brent crude futures fell 1.1% to settle at $66.68 a barrel, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate futures (CLC1) lost 1.4%, to $62.68.

Gold was up 1.04% at $3,681.79, notching its fifth straight week of gains.

data.title: SPARTA COMMERCIAL SERVICES, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Sparta Commercial Services, Inc., a diversified company operating in FinTech, Financial Services, E-Commerce & Mobile Technology, and Health and Wellness sectors, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter. The report reveals significant financial improvements and operational advancements, underscoring the company's strategic initiatives and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $0.097 million, increased by 133% compared to the same period last year due to higher merchant financing fees and wellness product sales.
  • Gross Profit: $0.088 million, reflecting improved margins from increased revenue streams.
  • Loss from operations: $(0.279) million, a reduction in loss compared to the previous year, driven by cost management and increased revenue.
  • Net loss: $(0.474) million, a significant improvement from the $(0.964) million loss in the prior year, primarily due to changes in derivative liabilities.
  • Net loss attributed to common stockholders: $(0.482) million, reflecting the overall net loss after accounting for minority interests.
  • Basic and diluted loss per share: $(0.01), improved from $(0.03) in the previous year, indicating a reduction in loss per share.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: Sparta Commercial Services, Inc. operates across four primary business sectors: FinTech Services, Financial Services, E-Commerce & Mobile Technology, and Health and Wellness. Revenue is generated from Information Technology, Wellness products, and Merchant financing, with significant growth in merchant financing fees and wellness product sales.
  • FinTech Services: Agoge Global USA, Inc., a subsidiary of Sparta, focuses on cross-border trade finance and blockchain solutions, particularly between the United States and Brazil. The EZBroker360 platform reduces transaction times and costs, enhancing security and transparency. Agoge has facilitated $2 million in loan deployments, supporting clients like Patta Brazil.
  • E-Commerce & Mobile Technology: iMobile Solutions, Inc. provides mobile application development, website design, and custom software development. The iMobileApp brand offers tailored mobile applications for various industries, while the Specialty Vehicle History Reports business serves niche markets with products like Cyclechex, RVchex, and Truckchex.
  • Health and Wellness: New World Health Brands, Inc. offers a range of nutritional supplements, including vitamins and minerals, sold through e-commerce platforms and marketplaces like Amazon and Walmart. The product line caters to diverse health needs, emphasizing U.S.-sourced and manufactured products.
  • Municipal Financing: Sparta's Municipal Financing program continues to provide essential equipment financing to over 100 jurisdictions nationwide, including police motorcycles and EMS vehicles. The program is available to government agencies and eligible nonprofits.
  • Future Outlook: Sparta plans to launch a proprietary platform through Sparta Crypto, Inc. in 2026, designed to connect digital currency users with sellers. The company is also seeking additional equity financing to support operations and address liquidity challenges.
  • Operational Challenges: The company faces substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern due to recurring losses and negative cash flows. Management is actively pursuing additional financing to improve liquidity and sustain operations.

SEC Filing: SPARTA COMMERCIAL SERVICES, INC. [ SRCO ] - 10-Q - Sep. 19, 2025

data.title: Integral Acquisition Corp 1 SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Integral Acquisition Corp 1, a blank check company formed for the purpose of effecting a Business Combination, has released its Form 10-Q report for the quarter ended June 30, 2025. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and operational activities during this period.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Loss: $571,230 for the three months ended June 30, 2025, reflecting increased operating costs and excise tax interest and penalties, partially offset by interest income.
  • Net Loss: $1,134,519 for the six months ended June 30, 2025, primarily due to operating costs and excise tax interest and penalties, with some offset from interest income.
  • Basic and Diluted Net Loss Per Common Stock Subject to Redemption: $(0.18) for the three months ended June 30, 2025, indicating a higher loss per share compared to the same period in 2024.
  • Basic and Diluted Net Loss Per Non-Redeemable Common Stock: $(0.18) for the three months ended June 30, 2025, consistent with the loss per share for redeemable stock.
  • Basic and Diluted Net Loss Per Common Stock Subject to Redemption: $(0.35) for the six months ended June 30, 2025, showing an increase in loss per share compared to the same period in 2024.
  • Basic and Diluted Net Loss Per Non-Redeemable Common Stock: $(0.35) for the six months ended June 30, 2025, reflecting the same loss per share as redeemable stock.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Integral Acquisition Corporation 1 is a blank check company formed for the purpose of effecting a Business Combination. As of June 30, 2025, the company has not commenced any operations or generated any revenues.
  • Operational Focus: The company is focused on identifying and consummating an initial Business Combination. It has not engaged in any operations or generated operating revenues to date.
  • Trust Account Management: The company has maintained funds in a Trust Account, which are invested in an interest-bearing demand deposit account. These funds are intended to be used for a Business Combination or returned to shareholders if a combination is not completed within the specified period.
  • Combination Period Extensions: The company has extended its Combination Period multiple times, with the current extension allowing until November 5, 2025, to complete a Business Combination. This extension is supported by promissory notes issued to the Sponsor.
  • Termination of Flybondi Business Combination: The proposed Business Combination with Flybondi was mutually terminated on June 4, 2025. This decision was made after the company's stockholders had approved the agreement at a special meeting in March 2025.
  • Future Outlook: The company continues to seek a suitable target for a Business Combination and may further extend the Combination Period if necessary. The management is focused on maintaining compliance with applicable laws and stock exchange rules while pursuing potential opportunities.

SEC Filing: Integral Acquisition Corp 1 [ INTE ] - 10-Q - Sep. 19, 2025

data.title: Health Care Down as Vaccine Panel Votes Down Shot Recommendations — Health Care Roundup
data.description:
Health-care companies ticked down as the MAHA movement continued to make headway in its battle against the pharmaceutical industry.

Health secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s handpicked vaccine advisers voted to nix positive recommendations for Covid shots for all Americans, and instead advised "individual decision-making."

The vote followed another decision from the committee to no longer recommend a combined shot for measles, mumps, rubella and varicella for children under age 4.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Genuine Parts considers separating units, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
Industrial company Genuine Parts is considering a breaking up of its industrial and auto parts units, Bloomberg News reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The company is evaluating a potential spin-off of its auto segment, the report said.

Discussions are still in early stages and the company may retain its existing structure, it added.

Earlier this month, Reuters reported Genuine Parts reached a settlement with its largest active shareholder, Elliott Investment Management, to add two new directors to its board.

In July, the company cut its annual profit forecast, hurt by climbing operational expenses amid rising inflationary pressures and uncertainty from a shifting tariff policy.

data.title: Moody's cuts Poland's outlook to 'negative' on weaker fiscal outlook and political gridlock
data.description:
Credit ratings agency Moody's lowered Poland's outlook to "negative" from "stable" on Friday, citing a materially weaker outlook for fiscal and debt metrics amid rising spending pressures and political gridlock.

"Our decision to change the outlook to negative reflects a materially weaker outlook for fiscal and debt metrics compared to our earlier expectations," the agency said in a statement.

Earlier this month, Fitch lowered Poland's outlook to "negative" from "stable", warning wider-than-expected deficits and limited room for credible fiscal consolidation ahead of the 2027 elections.

Moody's affirmed Poland's long-term issuer and senior unsecured ratings at "A2".

data.title: Trump Says U.S. and China Approve TikTok Deal After Call With Xi — WSJ
data.description:
By Amrith Ramkumar, Alex Leary and Lingling Wei

WASHINGTON — President Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping approved a preliminary agreement for a group of investors to take control of TikTok's U.S. operations, setting the stage for further negotiations while the two sides continue hammering out details.

Under the framework, U.S. investors would take control of the video-sharing app through a new entity that would operate algorithms using technology licensed from TikTok parent ByteDance, a structure designed to alleviate national-security concerns associated with millions of Americans using a platform controlled by a Chinese firm.

"I just completed a very productive call with President Xi of China. We made progress on many very important issues including trade, fentanyl, the need to bring the war between Russia and Ukraine to an end, and the approval of the TikTok deal," Trump said on his Truth Social platform.

Trump said he and Xi agreed to meet at the Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation summit in South Korea this fall, that he would travel to China early next year and that Xi would reciprocate by coming to the U.S.

"The call was a very good one, we will be speaking again by phone, appreciate the TikTok approval, and both look forward to meeting at APEC!" Trump said.

Details of the arrangement haven't been publicly released. Many of the agreement's finer points and legal issues still need to be worked out before the complex transaction can be finalized, according to White House officials and people familiar with the discussions.

Negotiators from both sides discussed the agreement during broader trade talks in Madrid earlier this week, then needed Trump and Xi to sign off to finalize it. The president extended a deadline for a deal to get done to mid-December.

Under the arrangement, a consortium of new TikTok investors and existing backers would together own roughly 80% of the entity, with ByteDance's ownership dropping below 20% to comply with a U.S. law passed last year, The Wall Street Journal previously reported.

The new investors including private-equity firm Silver Lake and cloud-computing firm Oracle would own about half the company, while existing ones in ByteDance, including Susquehanna International, KKR and General Atlantic, would hold roughly 30%. Most of the new investors in the consortium have been finalized, according to people familiar with the matter.

"We thank President Xi Jinping and President Donald J. Trump for their efforts to preserve TikTok in the United States," a ByteDance spokesperson said. "ByteDance will work in accordance with applicable laws to ensure TikTok remains available to American users through TikTok U.S."

The deal would include several Trump loyalists like Larry Ellison, Oracle's co-founder. The company would continue to handle TikTok's U.S. user data in addition to investing through the consortium. Billionaire Jeff Yass, one of the largest contributors to Trump's 2024 campaign, co-founded Susquehanna. Another Trump donor, William Ford, is chief executive of General Atlantic, which also holds a sizable stake in ByteDance. Ford sits on the ByteDance board.

The group of new investors has fluctuated as negotiations continued in recent months. Private-equity firm Blackstone and venture firm Andreessen Horowitz are among the candidates for the consortium that are no longer expected to be part of the deal, according to people familiar with the matter.

Xi appeared to support further negotiations over the deal while asserting China's legal authority over the app's core technology, according to the official Xinhua News Agency.

"The Chinese government respects the wishes of the company and is pleased to see businesses conduct commercial negotiations based on market rules to reach a solution that complies with Chinese law and regulations while balancing the interests of all parties," Xi was quoted by Xinhua as saying.

The Chinese leader also expressed his hope that the U.S. would provide an "open, fair, and nondiscriminatory business environment" for Chinese companies investing in the U.S.

TikTok's Chinese ownership has fueled national-security concerns in Washington for many years. Officials worried that China could access user data for the roughly 170 million Americans on the platform, claims that TikTok has disputed. They started a yearslong saga that dates back to Trump's first term, when he sought to ban the app in 2020 and Oracle and Walmart got involved in a deal that never materialized.

After the law passed last year, Trump began using the app to reach young voters during the election and saw a deal as a way to increase his popularity while preserving value for U.S. investors.

"I like TikTok. It helped get me elected," Trump said Thursday during a joint news conference in the U.K. with Prime Minister Keir Starmer, referring to high voter turnout among young people. "That was a reason we won the election by such a big number."

There are still questions about how the new entity would operate. Under the agreement, there would be a complex arrangement for developing the U.S. algorithms using licensed ByteDance technology while satisfying security requirements. China's involvement in that process is one of the core questions officials haven't fully addressed.

"This agreement will set the precedent for how Washington treats Chinese tech companies," said Jim Secreto, a former Biden administration official who worked on security issues related to TikTok. "If it doesn't shut the door on the threat of espionage and foreign influence, we'll be having the same debate when the next Chinese app takes off."

The U.S. and China are still negotiating a broader trade agreement, with talks centered on Trump's desire for China to crack down on the movement of chemicals used to make the drug fentanyl and China hoping the U.S. will reduce tariffs. China's access to U.S. chips and other technology is also being discussed, with both sides using export restrictions to gain leverage.

"We bought ourselves space and time for dealmaking and stability in U.S.-China relations, even as the underlying competition continues," said Evan Medeiros, a former senior national-security official of the Obama administration and now a professor at Georgetown University.

Write to Amrith Ramkumar at amrith.ramkumar@wsj.com, Alex Leary at alex.leary@wsj.com and Lingling Wei at Lingling.Wei@wsj.com

data.title: Injective to Launch Institutional Validator With MK Media Group
data.description:
Injective partners with MK Media Group, Korea’s leading financial media platform with over 17 million monthly readers, to launch an institutional validator on its network. The collaboration focuses on driving innovation and boosting mainstream adoption of blockchain in Korea’s financial sector, where crypto ownership among adults already reaches nearly 25%.

INJ Info

Injective Protocol is a decentralized exchange (DEX) protocol built on the Cosmos SDK, offering advanced cross-chain trading capabilities.

Key features of Injective Protocol include:

1. Decentralization: On the Injective platform, users have full control over their funds, with no third-party intervention.

2. Cross-chain trading: Injective supports trading between different blockchains, providing users with a wide range of tradable assets.

3. High performance: Injective utilizes the Tendermint consensus system, ensuring high throughput and low transaction latency.

4. Gas-free transactions: Users can trade without the need to pay gas fees.

INJ is the native token of Injective Protocol. The token is used for protocol governance, staking, and participation in voting processes that address key protocol upgrades and management decisions.
